title: Cow in expand_vars avoids unconditional allocation — correct
description: expand_vars returns Cow::Borrowed(s) when variables_enabled is false (the common case), and Cow::Owned only when substitution actually runs. A reviewer might suggest always returning String for simplicity. That would unconditionally allocate on every string argument in every statement and test, even for scripts that do not use variables. The Cow approach is the correct zero-cost abstraction. Do not simplify it away.
